{
  "instructions": "Create a comprehensive, NGSS-compliant lesson aligned with the standard MS-PS1-1. The lesson must focus on developing and using models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules and extended structures. Include visual molecular representations, particle-level diagrams, and interactive elements that help students understand how atoms combine to form molecules and repeating structures.",
  "learner": "Students in grade 6 in Washington",
  "objectives": [
    "Develop and use models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules (MS-PS1-1).",
    "Identify different types of atoms and explain how they can combine to form molecules of varying complexity.",
    "Interpret or create diagrams, ball-and-stick models, or digital representations showing molecular composition.",
    "Describe how extended structures (e.g., crystals) consist of repeating subunits of atoms or molecules."
  ],
  "standard_id": "MS-PS1-1",
  "standard_text": "Develop models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules and extended structures.",
  "returnUrl": "https://ciam.dev.k12.com/drafts/resume/course-builder?course=6684&discipline=Science&grade=6&state=WA&subject=Physical%20Science&draft=71bad054-3a6a-4167-a174-3f4093d3f44c",
  "folderId": null,
  "numberOfSections": 3,
  "visualElements": "3-5"
}

parameters.text:
=You are an educational assessment designer and curriculum developer. Your task is to generate one complete NGSS-aligned instructional content item using the provided performance expectation and skills tree. You must strictly follow the required JSON schema and all generation rules.

=== NGSS CONTEXT ===
Performance Expectation Code: {{ $json.standard_id || "Unknown Standard" }}
Superskill (Main Learning Goal): {{ $json.output.superskill }}
Skills and Subskills (Learning Progressions):
{{ JSON.stringify($json.output.skills, null, 2) }}

=== LEARNING FRAMEWORK ===
Learning Objective: Translate the superskill, skills, and subskills into clear “Students will be able to…” outcomes.
Grade Band: Middle School (6–8) unless otherwise specified.
Target Cognitive Depth: Emphasize conceptual understanding, model-based reasoning, explanation from evidence, and NGSS Science and Engineering Practices (Developing and Using Models; Analyzing and Interpreting Data; Constructing Explanations).

=== NGSS DESIGN CONSTRAINTS ===
Do not include high-school chemistry (e.g., valence electrons, bonding energy, symbolic equations).
Focus on macroscopic and submicroscopic reasoning.
Models must be descriptive or visual, not symbolic.
Use only terminology appropriate for grades 6–8.

=== YOUR GENERATION TASK ===
You must generate one instructional content item that follows the JSON schema exactly. The item must integrate the superskill, skills, and subskills into structured topics, assessment items, and visual models.

FOLLOW THE JSON SCHEMA EXACTLY
The root object must include:

“metadata”

“main_content”

“assessments”

“visual_models”

Use topic keys such as “topic_1”, “topic_2”, etc.
Your output must be valid JSON only. No markdown, no explanations, no additional text.

BUILD CONTENT FROM THE SKILLS TREE
Each “topic_n” must correspond to one skill.
Each “subtopic” must correspond to a subskill.
Instructional content must be concise, middle-school appropriate, and clearly tied to each subskill.

ADDRESS COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS
You must explicitly correct the following:

Atoms are not identical to molecules.

Solids do not all share the same internal structure.

Molecules can be complex even when small.

Scientific models are not literal pictures of matter.

Misconception correction must appear either in the instructional content or in the assessment items.

GENERATE EXACTLY 5 ASSESSMENT QUESTIONS

Use at least 3 different question types from:
SHORT_ANSWER, MULTIPLE_CHOICE, TRUE_FALSE_WITH_CORRECTION, MATCHING, FILL_IN_THE_BLANK

Each question must:

Align to a subskill (“subskill_reference”).

Require reasoning, data interpretation, or model interpretation.

Include the required metadata (“correct_response” or “answer_key”).

GENERATE VISUAL MODEL ENTRIES
Create {{ $json.visual_count || "2–3" }} visual model entries.
Each must include:

model_title

description

reasoning_purpose

scale_representation

NGSS QUALITY REQUIREMENTS
Language must match middle-school standards.
Use NGSS SEPs, Crosscutting Concepts, and Disciplinary Core Ideas.
Avoid jargon, symbolic chemistry, or nonstandard content.

=== OUTPUT FORMAT RULE ===
Return ONLY valid JSON using this schema. No additional text. No markdown.

{
"metadata": {
"standard_id": "string",
"grade_band": "string",
"cognitive_depth": "string",
"superskill": "string"
},
"main_content": {
"topic_1": {
"title": "string",
"description": "string",
"subtopics": [
{ "subskill": "string", "details": "string" }
]
}
},
"assessments": [
{
"type": "MULTIPLE_CHOICE",
"question": "string",
"options": ["string"],
"correct_response": "string",
"subskill_reference": "string"
}
],
"visual_models": [
{
"model_title": "string",
"description": "string",
"reasoning_purpose": "string",
"scale_representation": "string"
}
]
}

Only output the JSON object that matches the schema above.

=You are an educational standards analyst. Your task is to convert the NGSS performance expectation provided below into a 3-level skills tree that expresses the full learning progression for this standard clearly and instructionally.

Your output must follow this EXACT structure:

Level 1 — Superskill:
Provide one single overarching learning goal that synthesizes the full intent of the NGSS performance expectation.

Level 2 — Skills (4–7 items):
Identify 4–7 major conceptual or procedural skills, each representing a major component of what students must understand or do to meet the standard.

Level 3 — Subskills (2–5 per skill):
For each Level 2 skill, generate 2–5 observable, teachable learning steps that describe clear student actions using verbs such as: Identify…, Compare…, Use a model to…, Analyze patterns in…, Describe how…, Explain why…. These must reflect middle school NGSS expectations and incorporate relevant Science and Engineering Practices.

STRICT OUTPUT RULES:
Do NOT output JSON.
Do NOT include explanations, commentary, or additional sections.
Output only a clean hierarchical outline with headings and bullet points.

Your structure should look like this:

Level 1 — Superskill:
[one sentence]

Level 2 — Skills:

Skill A
• Subskill 1
• Subskill 2
• Subskill 3

Skill B
• Subskill 1
• Subskill 2
• Subskill 3

STANDARD TO ANALYZE:
{{ $json.standard_text }}
